creator: dim@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 2011-01-26 17:55, Erwin Lansing wrote:
> and maybe some more from the below logs of stuff I marked as interesting,

I went through these one by one, comparing 'regular' head (with binutils
2.15), and the binutils-2.17 branch.


> mostly because they don't fail on any
> other build, but I can't promise that some may be false positive anyway:
>
> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/gauche-0.9.log

Confirmed to work on binutils 2.15, to fail on 2.17.  This is a weird
one, the port builds a temporary interpreter 'gosh' to finish the rest
of the port, which is apparently a form of Scheme.  However, that gosh
executable immediately crashes with a segfault, if it is all compiled
with binutils 2.17.

There seems to be something fishy going on in its internal hash tables,
or maybe with the Boehm garbage collector it is using.  I suspect this
is just a memory bug, which has not been noticed until now, simply by
chance.  I am still investigating this one.


> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/fastcrc-1.0.log

Confirmed to work on binutils 2.15, to fail on 2.17.  This seems to be
due to it compiling an assembly file with nasm; nasm sets the OSABI
field in the ELF header to "UNIX - System V", instead of "UNIX -
FreeBSD".

This fastcrc.o file is then linked into a libfcrc.so.1 file, using "ld
-shared", and due to the mismatch in ABI headers, it strips out all the
dynamic symbols.  This leads to the link failures later on:

fc.c:(.text+0x87): undefined reference to `ecrcpartial'
fc.c:(.text+0x9e): undefined reference to `pcrcpartial'
fc.c:(.text+0xdc): undefined reference to `pcrcfinish'

The best solution to this would be to fix nasm on FreeBSD to produce the
correct OSABI field in the ELF header.  I have attached a patch for
that, bu217-devel-nasm-1.diff.  Though this will need thorough testing
to see if other ports that use nasm break.  I expect more will be fixed
than broken by it, though.

There are two alternative quick fixes, for if we don't want to mess with
nasm: the first one just replaces 'ld' with 'gcc' when producing the
shared library; this creates the proper dynamic symbols, and linking
will then succeed.

The second one cleans up the rather crappy Makefile in general, and also
allows compilation with clang.  I'm not sure what is acceptable here,
this seems a *very* old port, and I doubt many people use it.

Diffs to fix: bu217-devel-fastcrc-1.diff
           or: bu217-devel-fastcrc-2.diff


> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/postgresql-server-9.0.2.log

Confirmed to work on binutils 2.15, to fail on 2.17, at least with the
default settings.  There is some weird problem with the gssapi libs,
which was also reported by Daniel Gerzo here:

http://archives.postgresql.org/pgsql-bugs/2010-10/msg00127.php

when he tried compiling it with gcc 4.5.  Indeed, when you turn off the
gssapi support, the port builds to completion.

After some analysis, it turns out this is caused by the use of the
--as-needed ld option when linking the postgres executable.  It links
libgssapi_krb5.so, but the functions it complains about are in a
dependency lib of that, libgssapi.so.

Apparently, in the following binutils commit:

http://sourceware.org/git/?p=binutils.git;a=commit;h=e86cc6a893680298e9eb305a91d9c52b26e9039a

the --as-needed behaviour was changed, so the dependencies of a certain
library are ignored, if the library itself is not needed.  E.g.
libgssapi_krb5.so does not contain any function used by the postgres
object files, thus it is discarded by --as-needed, together with its
dependent libraries!  And then the required functions are not found.

The proper fix would be done upstream, because now the configure script
first checks if linking -lgssapi_krb5 is enough to find some basic
gssapi functions, and only *after* this it checks for --as-needed
support, and adds those options to LDFLAGS.  If the configure script had
added those flags earlier, it would have found that -lgssapi_krb5 was
not enough anymore.

In any case, it is probably simplest for us to hack configure a bit.

Diff to fix: bu217-databases-postgresql90-server-1.diff


> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/dri-7.4.4,2.log
> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/libGL-7.4.4.log

Hmm, both of these fail with bad patches:

Ignoring previously applied (or reversed) patch.
1 out of 1 hunks ignored--saving rejects to src/mesa/x86-64/glapi_x86-64.S.rej

This does not happen here?  Are those patches screwed? :)


> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/dsss-0.78.log

Fails both with binutils 2.15 and 2.17, so this is not a binutils 2.17
problem.  It dies on an undefined reference to 'log2l'.  This looks like
some problem with built-in log2l as opposed to it being in an external
library.


> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/glide3-v5-20020113_6.log

Confirmed to work on binutils 2.15, to fail on 2.17.  This dies because
it attempts to define the same macro twice in one .s file, which is not
allowed anymore with newer versions of as.  Solution is a simple #ifndef
guard, also described here:

http://bugs.gentoo.org/show_bug.cgi?format=multiple&id=114558

Diff to fix: bu217-graphics-glide3-1.diff


> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/sather-1.2.2_2.log

Confirmed to work on binutils 2.15, to fail on 2.17.  I think this may
have something to do with the Boehm garbage collector, as it dies with:

Initializing... Duplicate large block deallocation of 0x82b4000
Duplicate large block deallocation

It looks like /usr/local/lib/libgc-redirect.so.1 overrides malloc and
free, and something attempts a double free.  When I tested the
devel/boehm-gc-redirect port with binutils 2.15, I got 1 of 6 tests
failed, but with binutils 2.17, I got 2 of 6 tests failed.

There is definitely something fishy with boehm-gc... :(


> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/TenDRA-4.20051112.log

Fails both with binutils 2.15 and 2.17, so this is not a binutils 2.17
problem.  It dies on both with:

"/usr/include/machine/_types.h", line 61: Error:
   [ISO C90 6.5.2]: Can't use type specifiers 'long long' with '__extension__'.


> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/bsnmp-ucd-0.3.2.log

Fails both with binutils 2.15 and 2.17, so this is not a binutils 2.17
problem.  It dies on both with:

In file included from snmp_ucd.h:33,
                  from snmp_ucd.c:30:
/usr/include/bsnmp/snmpmod.h:365: error: expected specifier-qualifier-list before 'SLIST_ENTRY'
cc1: warnings being treated as errors


> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/qbittorrent-2.6.1.log

Hmm, strange, this port builds fine for me on both head and binutils
2.17.  Though my port is qbittorrent 2.6.3, so maybe this has just been
a transient problem.


> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/coco-2.3_1.log

Confirmed to work on binutils 2.15, to fail on 2.17.  It looks like this
is based on some version of emacs, which does *very* weird stuff with
linking.  For some reason, the internal gmalloc implementation goes
haywire, and causes a recursive stack fault at the first malloc call,
but only when using 2.17.


> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/pftop-0.7_1.log

We already found this problem earlier, it dies on both binutils 2.15 and
2.17.  It is due to the recent libpcap upgrade in the base system, which
causes pcap-int.h to override strlcpy, snprintf and vsnprintf with
'private' copies.  This leads to link errors.

Diff to fix: bu217-sysutils-pftop-1.diff


> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/driglide-0.9.1_7.log

Confirmed to work on binutils 2.15, to fail on 2.17.  This is
approximately the same problem as with the glide3 port above: it
redeclares the same macro multiple times in an assembly file.

Diff to fix: bu217-x11-servers-driglide-1.diff


> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/postgresql-server-8.4.6.log

Confirmed to work on binutils 2.15, to fail on 2.17; this is exaxctly
the same problem as with the postgres 9 port, above.

Diff to fix: bu217-databases-postgresql84-server-1.diff

Similar diffs may also be applied to any other postgresql port, I guess.


> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/gmfsk-0.6_10.log

My god, this port built about all other ports as dependencies. :)  In any
case, it fails both with binutils 2.15 and 2.17, with:

In file included from misc.c:25:
misc.h:110: error: conflicting types for 'log2'

So this is not a binutils 2.17 problem.  It looks like gcc chokes on the
declaration of log2, because it has this function built-in, and the
declaration in the header file misc.h is incompatible.  This can be
fixed easily, by renaming the 'log2' function to 'log2i'.

Diff to fix: bu217-comms-gmfsk-1.diff
